content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


September 28, 1994

Barbara Dunham
Chief of Staff
Office of Assemblyman Ted Weggeland
State Capitol
P.O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0001

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-94-292

Dear Ms. Dunham:

This letter confirms the telephone advice you received from
this office regarding Assemblyman Weggeland's travel arrangements
in Japan.

You related that Assemblyman Weggeland is presently in Japan
attending a conference sponsored by agencies of the government of
Japan. The Assemblyman's travel and accommodations for this
conference are paid by those government agencies. The Assemblyman
has been asked to attend further conferences with local officials
in the Osaka prefecture. You inquired whether or not the
Assemblyman's acceptance of this further offer of travel and
accommodation would be prohibited under the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1

The Assemblyman's acceptance of travel and accommodations
from agencies of the government of Japan for the purpose of
continuing his trip to Osaka would not violate the provisions of
the Act. Section 89506 exempts this type of travel from the $270
annual gift limit from any single source. Nevertheless, the gift
is still reportable by Assemblyman Weggeland on his annual
statement of economic interests. We enclose a copy of the
applicable regulatory provisions.


1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-292
Page 2


If you have any further questions regarding application of
the Act's provisions to Assemblyman Weggeland, this office remains
available to assist you.


Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Daniel E. Muallem
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures

sections.question: Whether or not the Assemblyman's acceptance of travel and accommodation from agencies of the government of Japan for further conferences in Osaka prefecture would be prohibited under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion: The Assemblyman's acceptance of travel and accommodations from agencies of the government of Japan for the purpose of continuing his trip to Osaka would not violate the provisions of the Act. Section 89506 exempts this type of travel from the $270 annual gift limit from any single source. Nevertheless, the gift is still reportable by Assemblyman Weggeland on his annual statement of economic interests.
sections.question_synthetic: Does acceptance of travel and accommodations from Japanese government agencies for conference attendance violate the Political Reform Act's gift provisions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The travel and accommodations from Japanese government agencies do not violate the Political Reform Act because Section 89506 exempts them from the $270 annual gift limit, though the gift must still be reported on the Assemblyman's annual statement of economic interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard FPPC advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document confirms prior telephone advice. All key statutory references included. Minor formatting irregularities in source document (spacing, line breaks) but content clarity is high.

QUESTION: Whether or not the Assemblyman's acceptance of travel and accommodation from agencies of the government of Japan for further conferences in Osaka prefecture would be prohibited under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The Assemblyman's acceptance of travel and accommodations from agencies of the government of Japan for the purpose of continuing his trip to Osaka would not violate the provisions of the Act. Section 89506 exempts this type of travel from the $270 annual gift limit from any single source. Nevertheless, the gift is still reportable by Assemblyman Weggeland on his annual statement of economic interests.
